MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Sep-18-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Philip Person, Esq. 415-655-1311 of Greenberg Traurig for Plaintiff.
James Parker, Esq. 503-778-5471 of Davis Wright Termaine for Defendant Sitzmann Morris & Lavis.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court makes a disclosure. Plaitniff argues issue of duty. Defendant responds. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF HORNBLOWER YACHTS, LLC., ALCATRAZ CRUISES, LLC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Judge Schulman discloses that while in private practice, he represented Hornblower Yachts, LLC during approximately the mid-1990s, and that until approximately 2008, he was a member of a law firm with Richard Jacobs, its vice president and general counsel. These former affiliations will not preclude Judge Schulman from being fair and impartial in deciding this matter.

Plaintiffs' motion for summary adjudication is denied on the ground that plaintiffs did not meet their initial burden of production in order to shift the burden to defendants. (Code Civ. Proc., 437c, subd. (f)(1); see Linden Partners v. Wilshire Linden Assocs. (1998) 62 Cal.App.4th 508, 518 ["The court may rule whether a defendant owes or does not owe a duty to plaintiff without regard for the dispositive effect of such ruling on other issues in the litigation, except that the ruling must completely dispose of the issue of duty."].) The existence of and scope of duty are legal questions for the court. (See Merill v. Navegar, Inc. (2001) 26 Cal.4th 465, 477.)

(see word minutes for full details)(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Counsel for Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with 3.1312.
